channel.title: IT-Security & Privacy News Aggregator
channel.description: Aggregierter RSS-Feed aus führenden IT-Security, Privacy und Cyber Policy Quellen

item.title: How to Comply with HECVAT in 2026 (Free Checklist) | UpGuard
item.description: If you're a vendor supporting the education sector, this checklist will help you prepare for any HECVAT questionnaires that come your way.

item.title: The Vendor Tiering Series: Mapping Tiers to Inherent Risk | UpGuard
item.description: Subjective labels do not categorize vendor risk. Learn how to build a defensible tiering architecture using objective criteria and a scalable model.

item.title: VMware Aria Operations Bug Exploited, Cloud Resources at Risk
item.description: Exploitation of the command injection flaw in VMware Aria Operations could grant an attacker broad acess to victims' cloud environments.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Perplexity AI Browser Flaw Could Let Calendar Invites Access Local Files
item.description: <p>Researchers say a vulnerability in Perplexity’s Comet AI browser could expose local files and credentials through malicious calendar invites.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-perplexity-comet-browser-vulnerability-local-files/">Perplexity AI Browser Flaw Could Let Calendar Invites Access Local Files</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Kezia Jungco

item.title: 149 Hacktivist DDoS Attacks Hit 110 Organizations in 16 Countries After Middle East Conflict
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a surge in retaliatory hacktivist activity following the U.S.-Israel coordinated military campaign against Iran, codenamed Epic Fury and Roaring Lion. "The hacktivist threat in the Middle East is highly lopsided, with two groups, Keymous+ and DieNet, driving nearly 70% of all attack activity between February 28 and March 2," Radware said in a Tuesday
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Stranger Things Meets Cybersecurity: Lessons from the Hive Mind
item.description: Events and concepts from the Stranger Things television series illustrate how enterprises can defend their networks and stay &quot;right side up.&quot;
item.dc:creator: Nadir Izrael

item.title: Coalition of Western Countries Launches 6G Cybersecurity Guidelines
item.description: A coalition of seven Western nations has launched guidelines to help integrate security-by-design principles into future 6G standards

item.title: Global Takedown Neutralizes Tycoon2FA Phishing Service
item.description: Law enforcers and industry partners have taken down notorious phishing-as-a-service platform Tycoon2FA

item.title: Google’s Biggest Android Security Update in Years Fixes 129 Bugs, Including an Actively Exploited Zero-Day
item.description: <p>Google’s March 2026 Android update patches 129 flaws, including an actively exploited Qualcomm zero-day, and urges users to install 2026-03-05.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-google-android-security-update-129-vulnerabilities/">Google’s Biggest Android Security Update in Years Fixes 129 Bugs, Including an Actively Exploited Zero-Day</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Aminu Abdullahi

item.title: Dark Reading Confidential: This Threat Hunter Helped Cops Bust Up An African Cybercrime Syndicate
item.description: Dark Reading Confidential Episode 15: Interpol relied on Will Thomas and team to help break up a sprawling cybercrime ring, leading to the arrest of 574 suspects, the recovery of more than $3 million, and the decryption of six malware variants. Here's his story.
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading Staff

item.title: Surge in Attacks on Surveillance Cameras Linked to Iranian Hackers
item.description: Increased attempts to compromise surveillance cameras linked to Iran during Middle East conflict

item.title: Multi-Stage "BadPaw" Malware Campaign Targets Ukraine
item.description: Malware campaign uses Ukrainian email service for credibility, deploying "BadPaw" to execute attacks

item.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Uses 23 Exploits Across Five Chains Targeting iOS 13–17.2.1
item.description: Google said it identified a "new and powerful" exploit kit dubbed Coruna (aka CryptoWaters) targeting Apple iPhone models running iOS versions between 13.0 and 17.2.1. The exploit kit featured five full iOS exploit chains and a total of 23 exploits, Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) said. It's not effective against the latest version of iOS. The findings were first reported by WIRED. "The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Manipulating AI Summarization Features
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: New RFP Template for AI Usage Control and AI Governance
item.description: As AI becomes the central engine for enterprise productivity, security leaders are finally getting the green light — and the budget — to secure it. But there’s a quiet crisis unfolding in the boardroom: many organizations know they need "AI Governance," but they have no idea what they are actually looking for. The CISO’s Dilemma: You Have the AI Budget, but Do You Have the Requirements? As AI
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: China's Silver Dragon Razes Governments in EU, SE Asia
item.description: The emerging actor, part of the APT41 nexus, gains initial access via phishing, and uses legitimate network services to obscure cyber espionage activities.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Calls for Global Digital Estate Standard as Posthumous Deepfake Fraud Risk Grows
item.description: The OpenID Foundation warns that fragmented policies on posthumous digital accounts could open the door for fraudsters to exploit AI deepfakes

item.title: Protecting education: How MDR can tip the balance in favor of schools
item.description: The education sector is notoriously short on cash, but rich in assets for threat actors to target. How can managed detection and response (MDR) help learning institutions regain the initiative?

item.title: Fake Laravel Packages on Packagist Deploy RAT on Windows, macOS, and Linux
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged malicious Packagist PHP packages masquerading as Laravel utilities that act as a conduit for a cross-platform remote access trojan (RAT) that's functional on Windows, macOS, and Linux systems. The names of the packages are listed below -
nhattuanbl/lara-helper (37 Downloads) nhattuanbl/simple-queue (29 Downloads) nhattuanbl/lara-swagger (49 Downloads)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: APT41-Linked Silver Dragon Targets Governments Using Cobalt Strike and Google Drive C2
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of an advanced persistent threat (APT) group dubbed Silver Dragon that has been linked to cyber attacks targeting entities in Europe and Southeast Asia since at least mid-2024. "Silver Dragon gains its initial access by exploiting public-facing internet servers and by delivering phishing emails that contain malicious attachments," Check Point said
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited VMware Aria Operations Flaw CVE-2026-22719 to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added a recently disclosed security flaw impacting Broadcom VMware Aria Operations to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing active exploitation in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-22719 (CVSS score: 8.1), has been described as a case of command injection that could allow an
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Indian APT 'Sloppy Lemming' Targets Defense, Critical Infrastructure
item.description: India-nexus cyber threat actors are growing more active and sophisticated, using custom tools coded in Rust and cloud-based command and control.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Vehicle Tire Pressure Sensors Enable Silent Tracking
item.description: Like many other features and systems in modern cars, tire pressure sensors leak sensitive data that can be abused by threat actors.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: Qualcomm Zero-Day Exploited in Targeted Android Attacks
item.description: The exploitation activity against CVE-2026-21385, a high-severity memory corruption flaw, could be tied to commercial spyware or nation-state threat groups.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: Announcing Risk Automations: From Discovery to Resolution In Seconds | UpGuard
item.description: Learn about UpGuard's new product launch. Transform your risk resolution engine by moving from alert to resolution in seconds, not days.

item.title: Speakeasies to Shadow AI: Banning AI Browsers Will Fail
item.description: Lessons from history highlight why AI-enabled browsers require controlled enablement.
item.dc:creator: Or Eshed

item.title: Chrome Extension Hijacked to Deliver Malware, Steal Crypto Wallets
item.description: <p>A compromised Chrome extension with 7,000 users was updated to deploy malware, strip security headers, and steal cryptocurrency wallet seed phrases.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-compromised-chrome-extension-malware-crypto-theft/">Chrome Extension Hijacked to Deliver Malware, Steal Crypto Wallets</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Fake Tech Support Spam Deploys Customized Havoc C2 Across Organizations
item.description: Threat hunters have called attention to a new campaign as part of which bad actors masqueraded as fake IT support to deliver the Havoc command-and-control (C2) framework as a precursor to data exfiltration or ransomware attack. The intrusions, identified by Huntress last month across five partner organizations, involved the threat actors using email spam as lures, followed by a phone call from
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Hacktivists Claim DHS Breach, Leak 6,600+ ICE Contractor Records
item.description: <p>Hacktivists claim they breached DHS systems, leaking records tied to 6,681 ICE contractor applicants, including major tech and defense firms.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-dhs-ice-contractor-data-leak-hacktivist-claim/">Hacktivists Claim DHS Breach, Leak 6,600+ ICE Contractor Records</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: Israel: RedAlert Spyware Campaign Exploits Wartime Panic With Trojanized App
item.description: Espionage campaign exploits Israel-Iran conflict, distributing a trojanized Red Alert app via SMS

item.title: AI and Deepfakes Supercharge Sophisticated Cyber-Attacks, Says Cloudflare
item.description: Cloudflare Threat Report warns that AI tools enable attackers who lacked required skills to generate effective attacks rapidly and at scale

item.title: Proofpoint Launches AI-Enabled Discovery & Archive Suite for Small to Medium Businesses
Building a High-Impact Tier 1: The 3 Steps CISOs Must Follow
item.description: Every CISO knows the uncomfortable truth about their Security Operations Center: the people most responsible for catching threats in real time are the people with the least experience. Tier 1 analysts sit at the front line of detection, and yet they are also the most vulnerable to the cognitive and organizational pressures that quietly erode SOC performance over time. The Paradox at the Gate:
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Leaked Database Sheds Light on Iranian Crypto Sanctions Evasion
item.description: Ariomex database reveals potential sanctions evasion and capital transfers tied to Iranian actors

item.title: Open-Source CyberStrikeAI Deployed in AI-Driven FortiGate Attacks Across 55 Countries
item.description: The threat actor behind the recently disclosed artificial intelligence (AI)-assisted campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate appliances leveraged an open-source, AI-native security testing platform called CyberStrikeAI to execute the attacks. The new findings come from Team Cymru, which detected its use following an analysis of the IP address ("212.11.64[.]250") that was used by the suspected
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Half of US CISOs Work the Equivalent of a Six-Day Week
item.description: Seemplicity finds US security leaders work 11 or more extra hours per week

item.title: AI Agent Overload: How to Solve the Workload Identity Crisis
item.description: Workloads keep getting more complicated and organizations are struggling to keep up. So what's the play?
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: On Moltbook
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: As War Continues, Pro-Iranian Actors Launch Barrage of Cyberattacks
item.description: Iran and its supporters have taken to cyberspace to retaliate for US-Israeli military action, with an aim to cause economic and physical disruption.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: AI Agents: The Next Wave Identity Dark Matter - Powerful, Invisible, and Unmanaged
item.description: The Rise of MCPs in the Enterprise The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming a practical way to push LLMs from “chat” into real work. By providing structured access to applications, APIs, and data, MCP enables prompt-driven AI agents that can retrieve information, take action, and automate end-to-end business workflows across the enterprise. This is already showing up in production
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Starkiller Phishing Suite Uses AitM Reverse Proxy to Bypass Multi-Factor Authentication
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new phishing suite called Starkiller that proxies legitimate login pages to bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA) protections. It's advertised as a cybercrime platform by a threat group calling itself Jinkusu, granting customers access to a dashboard that lets them select a brand to impersonate or enter a brand's real URL. It also lets
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Huge “Shadow Layer” of Organizations Hit by Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Black Kite reveals 26,000 unnamed corporate victims linked to 136 third-party breaches

item.title: Iranian Cyber Threat Actor Targets Iraqi Government Officials in AI-Powered Campaign
item.description: Zscaler ThreatLabz assessed with medium to high confidence that an Iranian adversary targeted Iraq’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs in a new cyber-attack

item.title: Microsoft Warns OAuth Redirect Abuse Delivers Malware to Government Targets
item.description: Microsoft on Monday warned of phishing campaigns that employ phishing emails and OAuth URL redirection mechanisms to bypass conventional phishing defenses implemented in email and browsers. The activity, the company said, targets government and public-sector organizations with the end goal of redirecting victims to attacker-controlled infrastructure without stealing their tokens. It described
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Confirms CVE-2026-21385 in Qualcomm Android Component Exploited
item.description: Google on Monday disclosed that a high-severity security flaw impacting an open-source Qualcomm component used in Android devices has been exploited in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-21385 (CVSS score: 7.8), a buffer over-read in the Graphics component. "Memory corruption when adding user-supplied data without checking available buffer space," Qualcomm said in an advisory,
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: SloppyLemming Targets Pakistan and Bangladesh Governments Using Dual Malware Chains
item.description: The threat activity cluster known as SloppyLemming has been attributed to a fresh set of attacks targeting government entities and critical infrastructure operators in Pakistan and Bangladesh. The activity, per Arctic Wolf, took place between January 2025 and January 2026. It involves the use of two distinct attack chains to deliver malware families tracked as BurrowShell and a Rust-based
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Critical OpenClaw Vulnerability Exposes AI Agent Risks
item.description: The now-patched flaw is the latest in a growing string of security issues associated with the viral AI tool, which has seen rapid adoption among developers.
item.dc:creator: Jai Vijayan

item.title: The Tug-of-War Over Firewall Backlogs in the AI-Driven Development Era
item.description: Speed and security are historically clashing priorities, but with AI and automation, it's increasingly important that application developers and security teams get on the same page.
item.dc:creator: Arielle Waldman

item.title: 30 Alleged Members of 'The Com' Arrested in Project Compass
item.description: The global law enforcement crackdown, which began in January 2025, also identified nearly 180 members of the notorious cybercriminal collective.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: The AI Exchange: Innovators in Payment Security Featuring Checkout.com
item.dc:creator: amalone@pcisecuritystandards.org (Alicia Malone)

item.title: New Chrome Vulnerability Let Malicious Extensions Escalate Privileges via Gemini Panel
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched security flaw in Google Chrome that could have permitted attackers to escalate privileges and gain access to local files on the system. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-0628 (CVSS score: 8.8), has been described as a case of insufficient policy enforcement in the WebView tag. It was patched by Google in early January 2026
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Google Develops Merkle Tree Certificates to Enable Quantum-Resistant HTTPS in Chrome
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Chrome Unveils Plan For Quantum-Safe HTTPS Certificates
item.description: Google Chrome initiates quantum-resistant measures via Merkle Tree Certificates to secure HTTPS

item.title: Expect Iran to Launch Cyber-Attacks Globally, Warns Google Head of Threat Intel
item.description: John Hultquist suggests “aggressive” Iranian cyber attackers will target the US and its Gulf allies with plausibly deniable ransomware attacks, hacktivist campaigns and more

item.title: Hybrid Middle East Conflict Triggers Surge in Global Cyber Activity
item.description: Military strikes in the Middle East escalate cyber ops, raising spillover risks globally for firms

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: SD-WAN 0-Day, Critical CVEs, Telegram Probe, Smart TV Proxy SDK and More
item.description: This week is not about one big event. It shows where things are moving. Network systems, cloud setups, AI tools, and common apps are all being pushed in different ways. Small gaps in access control, exposed keys, and normal features are being used as entry points. The pattern becomes clear only when you see everything together. Faster scans, smarter misuse of trusted services, and steady
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Quantum-Resistant Data Diode Secures Sensitive Data on Edge Devices, Critical Systems
item.description: Forward Edge-AI's new Isidore Quantum is a compact, low-power hardware device designed to defend sensitive operational technology endpoints against future quantum attacks.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Schwartz

item.title: LLM-Assisted Deanonymization
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: How to Protect Your SaaS from Bot Attacks with SafeLine WAF
item.description:
Most SaaS teams remember the day their user traffic started growing fast. Few notice the day bots started targeting them. On paper, everything looks great: more sign-ups, more sessions, more API calls. But in reality, something feels off:
Sign-ups increase, but users aren’t activating. Server costs rise faster than revenue. Logs are filled with repeated requests from strange user agents.
If
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ClawJacked Bug Enables Covert AI Agent Hijacking
item.description: Oasis Security reveals how a new ClawJacked vulnerability could allow attackers to silently take over a victim’s OpenClaw agent

item.title: Ransomware Payments Decline 8% as Attacks Surge 50%
item.description: Chainalysis reveals a big surge in median ransomware payment size in 2025 despite overall drop in criminal revenue

item.title: APT28 Tied to CVE-2026-21513 MSHTML 0-Day Exploited Before Feb 2026 Patch Tuesday
item.description: A recently disclosed security flaw patched by Microsoft may have been exploited by the Russia-linked state-sponsored threat actor known as APT28, according to new findings from Akamai. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-21513 (CVSS score: 8.8), a high-severity security feature bypass affecting the MSHTML Framework. "Protection mechanism failure in MSHTML Framework allows an unauthorized
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Bug in Google's Gemini AI Panel Opens Door to Hijacking
item.description: Attackers could have exploited the vulnerability to escalate privileges, violate user privacy while browsing, and access sensitive resources.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 26 npm Packages Hiding Pastebin C2 for Cross-Platform RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new iteration of the ongoing Contagious Interview campaign, where the North Korean threat actors have published a set of 26 malicious packages to the npm registry. The packages masquerade as developer tools, but contain functionality to extract the actual command-and-control (C2) by using seemingly harmless Pastebin content as a dead drop resolver and
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ClawJacked Flaw Lets Malicious Sites Hijack Local OpenClaw AI Agents via WebSocket
item.description: OpenClaw has fixed a high-severity security issue that, if successfully exploited, could have allowed a malicious website to connect to a locally running artificial intelligence (AI) agent and take over control. "Our vulnerability lives in the core system itself – no plugins, no marketplace, no user-installed extensions – just the bare OpenClaw gateway, running exactly as documented," Oasis
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
item.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.
item.dc:creator: BrianKrebs

item.title: This month in security with Tony Anscombe – February 2026 edition
item.description: In this roundup, Tony looks at how opportunistic threat actors are taking advantage of weak authentication, unmanaged exposure, and popular AI tools

item.title: Thousands of Public Google Cloud API Keys Exposed with Gemini Access After API Enablement
item.description: New research has found that Google Cloud API keys, typically designated as project identifiers for billing purposes, could be abused to authenticate to sensitive Gemini endpoints and access private data. The findings come from Truffle Security, which discovered nearly 3,000 Google API keys (identified by the prefix "AIza") embedded in client-side code to provide Google-related services like
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Pentagon Designates Anthropic Supply Chain Risk Over AI Military Dispute
item.description: Anthropic on Friday hit back after U.S. Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth directed the Pentagon to designate the artificial intelligence (AI) upstart as a "supply chain risk." "This action follows months of negotiations that reached an impasse over two exceptions we requested to the lawful use of our AI model, Claude: the mass domestic surveillance of Americans and fully autonomous weapons," the
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Fishing in Peru
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Life Mirrors Art: Ransomware Hits Hospitals on TV &amp; IRL
item.description: HBO's &quot;The Pitt&quot; is showing audiences what a real Mississippi healthcare system is going through this week, thanks to a ransomware attack.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Cities Hosting Major Events Need More Focus on Wireless, Drone Defense
item.description: Major events like the FIFA World Cup need to look beyond traditional physical and cyber security to active and passive wireless threats, say experts.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: Flaw-Finding AI Assistants Face Criticism for Speed, Accuracy
item.description: Using AI to find security vulnerabilities holds significant promise, but the initial products fall short of the needs of enterprises and software developers, say experts.
item.dc:creator: Robert Lemos

item.title: DoJ Seizes $61 Million in Tether Linked to Pig Butchering Crypto Scams
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) this week announced the seizure of $61 million worth of Tether that were allegedly associated with bogus cryptocurrency schemes known as pig butchering. The confiscated funds were traced to cryptocurrency addresses used for the laundering of criminally derived proceeds stolen from victims of cryptocurrency investment scams, the department added. "Criminal
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 900+ Sangoma FreePBX Instances Compromised in Ongoing Web Shell Attacks
item.description: The Shadowserver Foundation has revealed that over 900 Sangoma FreePBX instances still remain infected with web shells as part of attacks that exploited a command injection vulnerability starting in December 2025. Of these, 401 instances are located in the U.S., followed by 51 in Brazil, 43 in Canada, 40 in Germany, and 36 in France. The non-profit entity said the compromises are likely
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: 5 Nations Alert: Critical Cisco Bug Used in Global Espionage Campaign
item.description: <p>Hackers exploited a critical Cisco SD-WAN flaw, prompting a rare joint warning from the US, UK, Australia, Canada, and New Zealand.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-cisco-sdwan-flaw-five-eyes-joint-warning/">5 Nations Alert: Critical Cisco Bug Used in Global Espionage Campaign</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Joseph Ofonagoro

item.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
item.description: It's become a standard practice for organizations to disclose the bare minimum about a data breach, or worse — not disclose the incident at all.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Montalbano

item.title: Malicious Go Crypto Module Steals Passwords, Deploys Rekoobe Backdoor
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a malicious Go module that's designed to harvest passwords, create persistent access via SSH, and deliver a Linux backdoor named Rekoobe. The Go module, github[.]com/xinfeisoft/crypto, impersonates the legitimate "golang.org/x/crypto" codebase, but injects malicious code that's responsible for exfiltrating secrets entered via terminal password
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: North Korea's APT37 Expands Toolkit to Breach Air-Gapped Networks
item.description: The security researchers from Zscaler ThreatLabz have also discovered five new tools deployed by the North Korean hacking group

item.title: Millions at Risk as Android Mental Health Apps Expose Sensitive Data
item.description: <p>Oversecured flagged 1,575 flaws in 10 Android health apps with 14.7M installs, putting chats, CBT notes, and mood logs at risk, per BleepingComputer.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-android-mental-health-apps-14-7-million-installs/">Millions at Risk as Android Mental Health Apps Expose Sensitive Data</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Liz Ticong

item.title: Claude Code Security Shows Promise, Not Perfection
item.description: Claude Code's introduction rippled across the stock market, but researchers and analysts say its impact was overstated, as they peel back the layers.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Culafi

item.title: ScarCruft Uses Zoho WorkDrive and USB Malware to Breach Air-Gapped Networks
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as ScarCruft has been attributed to a fresh set of tools, including a backdoor that uses Zoho WorkDrive for command-and-control (C2) communications to fetch more payloads and an implant that uses removable media to relay commands and breach air-gapped networks. The campaign, codenamed Ruby Jumper by Zscaler ThreatLabz, involves the deployment of malware
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Why Tehran’s Two-Tiered Internet Is So Dangerous
item.description: <p>Iran is <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/world/2026/jan/28/iran-appears-to-ease-internet-blackout">slowly emerging</a> from the <a href="https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/25/world/middleeast/iran-internet.html">most severe</a> communications blackout in its history and one of the longest in the world. Triggered as part of January&#8217;s government crackdown against citizen protests nationwide, the regime implemented an <a href="https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/2/irans-economy-falters-as-internet-shutdown-hits-people-businesses-hard">internet shutdown</a> that transcends the standard definition of internet censorship. This was not merely blocking social media or foreign websites; it was a total communications shutdown.</p> <p>Unlike previous Iranian internet shutdowns where Iran&#8217;s domestic intranet&#8212;the National Information Network (NIN)&#8212;remained functional to keep the banking and administrative sectors running, the 2026 blackout ...</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: Phishing Attacks Against People Seeking Programming Jobs
item.description: <p><a href="https://www.reversinglabs.com/blog/fake-recruiter-campaign-crypto-devs">This</a> is new. North Korean hackers are posing as company recruiters, enticing job candidates to participate in coding challenges. When they run the code they are supposed to work on, it installs malware on their system.</p> <p>News <a href="https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/fake-job-recruiters-hide-malware-in-developer-coding-challenges/">article</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schneier

item.title: UK Vulnerability Monitoring Service Cuts Unresolved Security Flaws by 75%
item.description: The UK government says its new Vulnerability Monitoring Service has cut unresolved security flaws by 75% and reduced cyber-attack fix times from nearly two months to just over a week

item.title: ‘Project Compass’ Cracks Down on ‘The Com’: 30 Members of Notorious Cybercrime Gang Arrested
item.description: International law enforcement operation led by Europol targets network of teenagers and young adults involved in ransomware attacks, extortion and other crimes

item.title: Trojanized Gaming Tools Spread Java-Based RAT via Browser and Chat Platforms
item.description: Threat actors are luring unsuspecting users into running trojanized gaming utilities that are distributed via browsers and chat platforms to distribute a remote access trojan (RAT). "A malicious downloader staged a portable Java runtime and executed a malicious Java archive (JAR) file named jd-gui.jar," the Microsoft Threat Intelligence team said in a post on X. "This downloader used PowerShell
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Mobile app permissions (still) matter more than you may think
item.description: Start using a new app and you’ll often be asked to grant it permissions. But blindly accepting them could expose you to serious privacy and security risks.

item.title: Europe’s ManoMano Hit: 38M Customer Records Compromised in Vendor Breach
item.description: <p>ManoMano is notifying 38 million customers after a third-party customer service breach exposed personal data, highlighting growing supply chain security risks.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-manomano-38m-third-party-data-breach/">Europe’s ManoMano Hit: 38M Customer Records Compromised in Vendor Breach</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: Ken Underhill

item.title: Meta Files Lawsuits Against Brazil, China, Vietnam Advertisers Over Celeb-Bait Scams
item.description: Meta on Thursday said it's taking legal action to tackle scams on its platforms by filing lawsuits against what it calls deceptive advertisers based in Brazil, China, and Vietnam. As part of the effort, the advertisers' methods of payment have been suspended, related accounts have been disabled, and the website domain names used to pull off the scams have been blocked. Concurrently, the social
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Marquis v. SonicWall Lawsuit Ups the Breach Blame Game
item.description: When a company gets breached through a third-party security vendor, who should bear responsibility? For one FinTech company, the answer is the firewall provider.
item.dc:creator: Nate Nelson

item.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Under Exploitation for 3 Years
item.description: The maximum-severity vulnerability CVE-2026-20127 was exploited by an unknown but sophisticated threat actor who left very little evidence behind.
item.dc:creator: Rob Wright

item.title: Aeternum C2 Botnet Stores Encrypted Commands on Polygon Blockchain to Evade Takedown
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new botnet loader called Aeternum C2 that uses a blockchain-based command-and-control (C2) infrastructure to make it resilient to takedown efforts. "Instead of relying on traditional servers or domains for command-and-control, Aeternum stores its instructions on the public Polygon blockchain," Qrator Labs said in a report shared with The
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: ChatGPT Criminals: AI Scams Target Dating and Legal Impersonators
item.description: <p>OpenAI says scammers are using ChatGPT to commit romance scams and impersonate law firms. Learn the fastest verification steps to avoid AI-assisted fraud.</p> <p>The post <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com/article/news-chatgpt-ai-scams-dating-legal/">ChatGPT Criminals: AI Scams Target Dating and Legal Impersonators</a> appeared first on <a href="https://www.techrepublic.com">TechRepublic</a>.</p>
item.dc:creator: TechRepublic Staff

item.title: Aeternum Botnet Shifts Command Control to Polygon Blockchain
item.description: New botnet Aeternum shifted C2 operations to Polygon blockchain, complicating takedown efforts

item.title: UAT-10027 Targets U.S. Education and Healthcare with Dohdoor Backdoor
item.description: A previously undocumented threat activity cluster has been attributed to an ongoing malicious campaign targeting education and healthcare sectors in the U.S. since at least December 2025. The campaign is being tracked by Cisco Talos under the moniker UAT-10027. The end goal of the attacks is to deliver a never-before-seen backdoor codenamed Dohdoor. "Dohdoor utilizes the DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH)
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Darktrace Flags 32 Million Phishing Emails in 2025 as Identity Attacks Intensify
item.description: 2025 saw 32M phishing emails, with identity threats surpassing vulnerabilities

item.title: Proofpoint Collaboration Security Integrates with New Extended Plan for AWS Security Hub
ThreatsDay Bulletin: Kali Linux + Claude, Chrome Crash Traps, WinRAR Flaws, LockBit & 15+ Stories
item.description:
Nothing here looks dramatic at first glance. That’s the point. Many of this week’s threats begin with something ordinary, like an ad, a meeting invite, or a software update. Behind the scenes, the tactics are sharper. Access happens faster. Control is established sooner. Cleanup becomes harder. Here is a quick look at the signals worth paying attention to.
AI-powered command
item.dc:creator: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

item.title: Exploitable Vulnerabilities Present in 87% of Organizations
item.description: Datadog report reveals two-fifths of services are affected by exploitable bugs
